findings.title: Revert of prior security fix reintroduces raw exception logging in validate_connection
findings.current_code:
logger.warning(
    "{app}: Unexpected HTTP error: {exc}",
    app=self._app_name,
    exc=exc,
)
findings.problem: CLAUDE.md (root, Deep Code Review Convention item 1) states: 'No API keys in logs, responses, or HTML. SecretStr discipline maintained.' The global CLAUDE.md security section also states: 'Never log sensitive data (passwords, tokens, PII, auth headers).' This diff is an exact, line-for-line revert of commit d47b4c2, titled 'fix(security): prevent potential key leakage in validate_connection log messages', which had deliberately replaced {exc} interpolation with {status} and {count} at these exact call sites specifically to address 'potential key leakage'. The working-tree diff silently reintroduces {exc} logging with no comment or commit message explaining why the prior, security-motivated fix is being undone.
findings.why_it_matters: The prior fix was authored specifically because logging the raw exception object was judged a key-leakage risk at this call site. Reverting it without acknowledgment removes that safety margin: a future httpx or pydantic version bump could change what str(exc) includes, silently reintroducing the exact issue already found and fixed once. Empirically verified (repo-pinned httpx 0.28.1, pydantic 2.12.5) that str(HTTPStatusError) currently contains only status code + URL, and the redacting sink would catch the key if it ever appeared -- so this is a regression-of-a-named-fix / process concern, not a confirmed live vulnerability.
findings.fix_hint: Either keep the pre-diff form (log {status}/{count} rather than the raw exception) to preserve the original security fix's intent, or, if reverting is intentional, add a code comment and commit message explicitly documenting that decision so it isn't silently lost again on the next refactor.

findings.title: Raw HTTPStatusError logging can leak URL credentials
findings.current_code:
logger.warning(
    "{app}: Unexpected HTTP error: {exc}",
    app=self._app_name,
    exc=exc,
)
findings.problem: The changed warning now formats the full httpx.HTTPStatusError. httpx includes the failed request URL in that exception string, and this repo's URL validation accepts http(s) URLs with userinfo while collect_secrets() only redacts configured API/auth secrets, not credentials embedded in the base URL. A configured reverse-proxy URL like https://user:pass@host would therefore write user:pass to stderr and the web log buffer on any non-401 validation failure. This is a regression from the prior status-code-only log.
findings.why_it_matters: Credentials embedded in a user-configured base URL (reverse-proxy userinfo) are not in the redaction secrets list, so they would land verbatim in stderr and the web-UI log buffer on any non-401 validation failure.
findings.fix_hint: Do not log exc directly. Keep logging exc.response.status_code, or add a shared sanitizer that logs status plus a URL with userinfo/query stripped and add a regression test proving URL credentials do not appear in validate_connection logs.
